proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=96, 94.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +34.3pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8815 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
R-multiple 0.61 reflects Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8; revisit after D-8 resolution as exit rule changes could materially shift cohort R distribution
Bear regime has been absent from the 7d window; when it returns, the cohort will activate into a portfolio currently 100% Choppy — monitor for regime transition compounding
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=96, wr=94.7%, edge +34.3pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=96, Wilson lower 0.8815, +34.3pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (stable, 7 days) with 100% Choppy exposure across 14 open positions, so activation is deferred and the existing concentration is not compounded. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-04-29 through 2026-05-26) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: UP_TRI × Metal: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, sector=Metal (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +27.9pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.72 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)

Portfolio is 100% Choppy-concentrated and UP_TRI dominates at 10/14; while Metal sector-narrowing helps, repeated promotion of UP_TRI-derived boosts risks compounding signal-type concentration if matches activate
R-multiple 0.72 reflects Day-6 forced-exit dominance (D-8 review); promotion to Tier M is appropriate but treat any uplift to Tier S as gated on D-8 resolution
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +27.9pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +27.9pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.72. The sector=Metal filter narrows rather than amplifies UP_TRI exposure (10 of 14 open) in a 100% Choppy-concentrated portfolio, and TAKE_SMALL sizing limits incremental concentration. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-04-29 through 2026-05-26) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_regime = Choppy (100.0%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_regime = Choppy; recommended action: Pause new entries in regime=Choppy until either (a) regime composition diversifies below 80% via natural Day-6 exits or (b) Choppy×UP_TRI or Choppy×BULL_PROXY cohort promotes to Tier M/S. Do not add new long signals to Auto, Metal, Energy, or Bank sectors until concentration eases.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A Choppy→Bear regime flip would unwind all 14 long positions simultaneously with no statistical edge validation to justify holding through the transition — fully directional long book has zero offsetting exposure.

evidence: regime_concentration.triggered=true, observed=1.0 vs threshold=0.80 (Choppy dominant across all 14 open positions)
Matched Choppy×UP_TRI cohort at Candidate tier only (n=118) — unvalidated edge
Matched Choppy×BULL_PROXY cohort at Candidate tier only (n=16) — unvalidated edge
Top cells (UP_TRI×Choppy×Auto, Metal, Energy; BULL_PROXY×Choppy×Bank) all at 14.29% — below 20% cell threshold but cluster broadly across the book
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in regime=Choppy until either (a) regime composition diversifies below 80% via natural Day-6 exits or (b) Choppy×UP_TRI or Choppy×BULL_PROXY cohort promotes to Tier M/S. Do not add new long signals to Auto, Metal, Energy, or Bank sectors until concentration eases.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A Choppy→Bear regime flip would unwind all 14 long positions simultaneously with no statistical edge validation to justify holding through the transition — fully directional long book has zero offsetting exposure.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: Portfolio is 100% Choppy regime (observed 1.0, above the 0.80 threshold) and all matched cohorts (Choppy×UP_TRI n=118, Choppy×BULL_PROXY n=16) sit only at Candidate tier — unvalidated edge. No single cell breaches 20% (top cells at 14.29%) and UP_TRI share at 10/14 = 71.4% is below 85%, but the regime concentration alone with unvalidated cohort support warrants warn — the book is also fully directional long (14/14), so a Choppy→Bear flip would unwind the entire book simultaneously with no statistical justification.
